skill.name: hybrid-cloud-networking
skill.description: Configure secure, high-performance connectivity between on-premises infrastructure and cloud platforms using VPN and dedicated connections. Use when building hybrid cloud architectures, connecting data centers to cloud, or implementing secure cross-premises networking.
skill.summary: Plan VPN, Direct Connect, ExpressRoute, and cloud interconnect designs for hybrid cloud networks.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown code fences, example network addresses, and legitimate cloud networking terminology. The skill is documentation-only and does not contain executable scripts, hidden network calls, credential access, or prompt injection text. Low residual risk remains because it includes example cloud CLI commands that users may run manually.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence Misidentified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported Ruby or shell backtick detections are Markdown code fences and documentation delimiters, not executable Ruby or shell evaluation. The skill does not include a script runner or instructions that automatically execute these blocks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line context shows Markdown fences, inline reference names, and related skill links rather than executable Ruby backticks. No file contains code that evaluates these strings.
low_findings.remediation: No remediation required for command execution. Keep examples clearly marked as documentation.

low_findings.title: Example Network Addresses Are Documentation Values
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The hardcoded IP findings are example network values used in Terraform and BGP documentation. The public address uses the reserved documentation range, and the private CIDR is a common route advertisement example.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The addresses appear in clearly labeled cloud networking examples, not in executable connection logic. The surrounding content describes architecture guidance.
low_findings.remediation: No security remediation required. Users should replace sample addresses with their own approved network ranges.

low_findings.title: Cloud Networking Terms Misidentified as Malware Indicators
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The C2, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance flags map to legitimate hybrid networking concepts such as IPSec VPN, cloud inventory commands, monitoring metrics, routing, and packet loss troubleshooting. No evidence found of command-and-control behavior, malware staging, or unauthorized discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious terms occur in normal cloud VPN, routing, and monitoring guidance. The content does not request stealth, persistence, credential theft, or unauthorized access.
low_findings.remediation: No malware remediation required. Review cloud command examples before use in production accounts.

content.value_statement: Hybrid cloud projects need reliable links between data centers and cloud networks. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan VPN, dedicated connectivity, routing, security, availability, and cost tradeoffs.
actual_capabilities: Compares VPN, AWS Direct Connect, Azure ExpressRoute, and GCP Cloud Interconnect options.
Explains hub-and-spoke, multi-region, and multi-cloud hybrid network patterns.
Provides Terraform-style examples for AWS and Azure VPN gateway resources.
Covers BGP route propagation, route filtering, and advertised network ranges.
Lists monitoring metrics for tunnel status, packet loss, latency, and BGP sessions.
Summarizes security, high availability, and cost optimization practices.
limitations: It is guidance content, not an automated deployment tool.
It does not validate live cloud account settings or current provider quotas.
Example addresses, AS numbers, and resource names must be replaced before use.
It does not include detailed provider-specific runbooks beyond the visible examples.

use_cases.title: Plan a Data Center to Cloud Link
use_cases.description: Compare VPN and dedicated connectivity for latency, bandwidth, reliability, and cost requirements.

use_cases.title: Prepare Routing and Failover Guidance
use_cases.description: Create BGP, route propagation, and dual-tunnel recommendations for a hybrid network design.

use_cases.title: Review Hybrid Network Security Controls
use_cases.description: Check whether a proposed design includes encryption, private endpoints, flow logs, DDoS protection, and audits.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Connection Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a first hybrid connection.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare site-to-site VPN, AWS Direct Connect, Azure ExpressRoute, and GCP Cloud Interconnect for my hybrid cloud project.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Hub-and-Spoke Network
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a hub-and-spoke hybrid network that connects one data center to production, staging, and development cloud networks.

prompt_templates.title: Review BGP and Routing
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my hybrid cloud BGP plan. Include AS number choices, route advertisement, route filtering, and failover considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Operations Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a production readiness checklist for hybrid cloud networking, covering security, monitoring, high availability, and cost controls.

output_examples.input: Which connection should we use for a 500 Mbps migration link?
output_examples.output: A VPN can work for short-term or budget-sensitive migration traffic. Dedicated connectivity is better when latency, reliability, or sustained throughput are critical.

output_examples.input: What should I monitor after deploying site-to-site VPN?
output: BGP session state and route advertisements.
Cloud flow logs and security alerts.

output_examples.input: How should we improve availability for hybrid connectivity?
output_examples.output: Use dual tunnels, separate physical locations where possible, dynamic routing with BGP, and continuous monitoring for tunnel health.

best_practices: Replace every sample address, AS number, and resource name with approved production values.
Use private connectivity and private endpoints for sensitive or high-volume workloads.
Validate routing, security groups, network ACLs, and flow logs before production traffic cutover.
anti_patterns: Do not copy example Terraform snippets directly into production without review.
Do not rely on a single VPN tunnel for critical hybrid workloads.
Do not advertise broad routes without documented filtering and approval.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy cloud networking resources?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples, but users must run and review any deployment steps.

faq.question: Which cloud providers does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers AWS, Azure, and GCP hybrid connectivity concepts and examples.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use this for Terraform planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help explain and adapt the included Terraform-style VPN examples.

faq.question: Does it include security guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers encryption, private endpoints, network ACLs, flow logs, DDoS protection, and audits.

faq.question: Are the IP addresses ready for production?
faq.answer: No. They are examples and must be replaced with approved addresses from your environment.

faq.question: Does it replace provider documentation?
faq.answer: No. Use it as planning guidance, then verify final settings against current provider documentation.
